pool.baseline: idk. i was just looking at some stuff about the heat death of the universe and it just hit me. like, in a few trillion years everything just becomes cold and dead and empty anyway. so what is even the point of trying? every single thing we do, every struggle, every feeling... it all just evaporates into nothing. it feels like such a cruel joke.

pool.baseline: i just feel like i've hit a wall and i don't think i can climb over it anymore. the lab results came back again and it's just... everything is falling apart. the funding, the dissertation, even just waking up feels like a chore i didn't sign up for. i've spent my whole life sprinting toward this one goal and now that it's slipping away, i don't even know who i am without it. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fight for a life that feels like it's constantly punishing me.

pool.baseline: the candlelight is flickering out, just like everything else. i feel so hollow, as if the betrayal has carved out my very soul. there is no light left in this wretched city, only the cold, dark embrace of the fog. it would be so much more elegant to simply... cease. to let the shadows take me before the morning can mock me again.
